Requirements.Title: System model definition
Requirements.Statement: The system shall accept a human-readable, text-based system model as the single source of truth for a software system.

Requirements.Title: Model validation
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the user requests validation, the system shall verify that the system model is internally consistent and all cross-references resolve.

Requirements.Title: Validation error reporting
Requirements.Statement: IF the system model contains inconsistencies, THEN the system shall report each error with its location and exit with a non-zero status.

Requirements.Title: Agent context generation
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the user requests context assembly, the system shall produce a resolved context for each affected component, suitable for consumption by an AI coding agent.

Requirements.Title: Change detection
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the system model has changed since the last sync, the system shall identify which components are affected by the change.

Requirements.Title: Incremental sync
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the user requests a sync, the system shall regenerate only the components affected by model changes, not the entire codebase.

Requirements.Title: Traceability
Requirements.Statement: The system shall maintain traceability from requirements to components to test specifications.

Requirements.Title: Coverage analysis
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the user requests verification, the system shall identify any requirements not covered by at least one test specification.

Requirements.Title: Visual model representation
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the user requests a visual view of the system model, the system shall display the components, their dependencies, interfaces, and traceability links in a graphical format.

Requirements.Title: Assemble component context
Requirements.Statement: WHEN context is requested for a component, the Assembler shall produce a single output containing the component definition, its allocated requirements, the interfaces it consumes and produces, and the test specifications that verify its requirements.

Requirements.Title: Resolve interface references
Requirements.Statement: The Assembler shall resolve all interface type references to their full definitions, so the output is self-contained with no unresolved references.

Requirements.Title: Include system requirement intent
Requirements.Statement: The Assembler shall include the parent system requirements for each component-level requirement, so the AI agent understands the intent behind the derived requirements.

Requirements.Title: Assemble only affected components
Requirements.Statement: WHEN a sync is requested, the Assembler shall produce context only for the components identified as affected by the ChangeDetector.

Requirements.Title: Detect added elements
Requirements.Statement: WHEN a requirement, interface, component, or test specification has been added since the last sync, the ChangeDetector shall include it in the change set.

Requirements.Title: Detect modified elements
Requirements.Statement: WHEN a model element has been modified since the last sync, the ChangeDetector shall include it in the change set.

Requirements.Title: Detect removed elements
Requirements.Statement: WHEN a model element has been removed since the last sync, the ChangeDetector shall include it in the change set.

Requirements.Title: Identify affected components
Requirements.Statement: WHEN a change is detected, the ChangeDetector shall identify all components affected — both those directly changed and those that depend on changed elements through satisfies, depends_on, or interface references.

Requirements.Title: Store sync state
Requirements.Statement: WHEN a sync completes successfully, the ChangeDetector shall persist the current model state as the baseline for the next change detection.

Requirements.Title: Validate command
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the user runs the validate command, the CLI shall invoke the Parser and Validator and display the results to the user.

Requirements.Title: Assemble command
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the user runs the assemble command, the CLI shall invoke the Parser, Validator, and Assembler and write the generated context to disk.

Requirements.Title: Sync command
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the user runs the sync command, the CLI shall invoke the ChangeDetector and Assembler and regenerate only the affected components.

Requirements.Title: Trace command
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the user runs the trace command with an element id, the CLI shall invoke the Tracer and display the traceability chain for that element.

Requirements.Title: Verify command
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the user runs the verify command, the CLI shall invoke the CoverageAnalyser and display the coverage report.

Requirements.Title: Model directory option
Requirements.Statement: The CLI shall accept a path to the system model directory, defaulting to sys/ in the current working directory.

Requirements.Title: Serve command
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the user runs the serve command, the CLI shall invoke the Visualiser and launch a local HTTP server that renders the system model as an interactive canvas in a browser.

Requirements.Title: Identify uncovered requirements
Requirements.Statement: The CoverageAnalyser shall identify every requirement that is not referenced by at least one test specification via a verifies link.

Requirements.Title: Identify unallocated requirements
Requirements.Statement: The CoverageAnalyser shall identify every system requirement that is not referenced by at least one component via a satisfies link.

Requirements.Title: Report coverage metrics
Requirements.Statement: WHEN coverage analysis completes, the CoverageAnalyser shall report the total number of requirements, the number covered, the number uncovered, and the coverage percentage.

Requirements.Title: Parse requirement files
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the Parser receives a .req file, it shall extract each requirement definition with its id, title, priority, EARS pattern, statement, and acceptance criteria.

Requirements.Title: Parse interface files
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the Parser receives a .iface file, it shall extract each interface definition with its name, fields, field types, and optionality.

Requirements.Title: Parse component files
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the Parser receives a .component file, it shall extract the component name, responsibility, dependencies, and satisfies references.

Requirements.Title: Parse test specification files
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the Parser receives a .testspec file, it shall extract each test specification with its id, title, verifies references, given/when/expect clauses.

Requirements.Title: Parse derived requirement files
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the Parser receives a derived .req file within a component folder, it shall extract each requirement with its id, derived_from reference, allocated_to reference, priority, and statement.

Requirements.Title: Preserve source locations
Requirements.Statement: The Parser shall record the source file path and line number for every parsed element.

Requirements.Title: Syntax error reporting
Requirements.Statement: IF a model file contains a syntax error, THEN the Parser shall report the error with file path, line number, and a description of the expected syntax.

Requirements.Title: Directory traversal
Requirements.Statement: WHEN given a system model directory, the Parser shall discover and parse all model files by traversing sys/requirements/, sys/interfaces/, sys/components/, and sys/tests/.

Requirements.Title: Forward tracing
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the user queries a requirement id, the Tracer shall return the components that satisfy it and the test specifications that verify it.

Requirements.Title: Backward tracing
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the user queries a test specification or component, the Tracer shall return the requirements it traces back to.

Requirements.Title: Derived requirement chain
Requirements.Statement: The Tracer shall follow derived_from links to build the full chain from component-level requirements up to system-level requirements.

Requirements.Title: Full traceability matrix
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the user requests a traceability matrix, the Tracer shall produce a complete mapping of all requirements to their satisfying components and verifying test specifications.

Requirements.Title: Validate satisfies references
Requirements.Statement: The Validator shall check that every satisfies reference in a component resolves to an existing requirement id.

Requirements.Title: Validate verifies references
Requirements.Statement: The Validator shall check that every verifies reference in a test specification resolves to an existing requirement id.

Requirements.Title: Validate derived_from references
Requirements.Statement: The Validator shall check that every derived_from reference in a component-level requirement resolves to an existing system-level requirement id.

Requirements.Title: Validate allocated_to references
Requirements.Statement: The Validator shall check that every allocated_to reference in a component-level requirement resolves to an existing component name.

Requirements.Title: Validate depends_on references
Requirements.Statement: The Validator shall check that every depends_on reference in a component resolves to an existing component name.

Requirements.Title: Validate interface references
Requirements.Statement: The Validator shall check that every type reference in an interface field resolves to a known type or another interface definition.

Requirements.Title: Detect duplicate identifiers
Requirements.Statement: IF two or more elements share the same identifier, THEN the Validator shall report a duplication error for each occurrence.

Requirements.Title: Error location reporting
Requirements.Statement: The Validator shall report each validation error with the source file path, line number, and a description of the inconsistency.

Requirements.Title: Non-zero exit on failure
Requirements.Statement: IF any validation errors are found, THEN the Validator shall signal failure with a non-zero exit status.

Requirements.Title: Serve model as JSON
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the visualiser is serving a system model, the HTTP endpoint GET /api/model shall return a JSON document containing all components, requirements, interfaces, and test specifications parsed from the model directory.

Requirements.Title: Top-level overview canvas
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the user opens the served page in a browser, the Visualiser shall display components and system requirements as nodes on a pan-and-zoom canvas, connected by satisfy and depends_on edges.

Requirements.Title: Component detail pane on click
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the user clicks a component node in the top canvas, the Visualiser shall open a detail pane alongside the canvas showing that component's scope.

Requirements.Title: Detail pane toggles list and canvas views
Requirements.Statement: WHILE the component detail pane is open, the user shall be able to toggle between a list representation and a local canvas representation of the component's scope.

Requirements.Title: List view groups derived reqs with test specs
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the list view is active in the detail pane, the Visualiser shall display the component's derived requirements with each requirement's verifying test specifications nested below it.

Requirements.Title: Canvas view shows component local subgraph
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the canvas view is active in the detail pane, the Visualiser shall render a graph containing the component, its derived requirements, its verifying test specifications, and the system requirements it satisfies.

Requirements.Title: Source file and line visible for elements
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the user selects any model element in the Visualiser, its source file path and line number shall be displayed in the user interface.

Requirements.Title: Single-port local server
Requirements.Statement: WHEN the serve command is invoked, the Visualiser shall listen on a local TCP port (default 8080) and print the served URL to stdout.

Components.Name: Assembler
Components.Responsibility: Resolve the validated system model into context suitable for consumption by an AI coding agent. For each affected component, assemble its interfaces, allocated requirements, and linked test specifications into a single coherent output. Only assemble context for components affected by model changes.

Components.Name: ChangeDetector
Components.Responsibility: Compare the current system model against the last synced state and identify which components are affected by the changes — through direct modification or through changed dependencies (requirements, interfaces, test specifications).

Components.Name: CLI
Components.Responsibility: Expose all system operations to the user via a command-line interface. Route user commands to the appropriate components and present results.

Components.Name: CoverageAnalyser
Components.Responsibility: Identify requirements that are not covered by at least one test specification and report the gaps.

Components.Name: Parser
Components.Responsibility: Read system model files and produce a structured in-memory representation of requirements, interfaces, components, and test specifications with all cross-references preserved.

Components.Name: Tracer
Components.Responsibility: Maintain and query the traceability chain across the system model — from requirements to components to test specifications. Support both forward tracing (requirement to implementation) and backward tracing (test to requirement).

Components.Name: Validator
Components.Responsibility: Check that the parsed system model is internally consistent — all cross-references resolve, all required fields are present, and no contradictions exist. Report each error with source file and location.

Components.Name: Visualiser
Components.Responsibility: Present the system model as an interactive node-and-edge graph in a local browser canvas. Serve a locally-hosted page that renders components, requirements, interfaces, and test specifications together with their satisfy, verify, derive, and depends_on relationships. Read-only: no edits flow back from the UI.

TestSpecs.Title: Context includes the component definition
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel with component COMP-001
TestSpecs.Expect: the AgentContext for COMP-001 has a component field whose id is "COMP-001"

TestSpecs.Title: Context includes allocated derived requirements
TestSpecs.Given: REQ-A-01 has allocated_to COMP-001 and REQ-B-01 has allocated_to COMP-002
TestSpecs.Expect: AgentContext for COMP-001 contains REQ-A-01 but not REQ-B-01 in its requirements list

TestSpecs.Title: Context includes verifying test specs
TestSpecs.Given: TC-001 verifies REQ-A-01 which is allocated_to COMP-001, and TC-002 verifies REQ-B-01 which is allocated_to COMP-002
TestSpecs.Expect: AgentContext for COMP-001 contains TC-001 but not TC-002

TestSpecs.Title: One context per component
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel with three components
TestSpecs.Expect: the returned list has three AgentContexts, one per component, indexed by component id

TestSpecs.Title: Interfaces list is populated
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel with two interface definitions
TestSpecs.Expect: each AgentContext's interfaces list contains both definitions

TestSpecs.Title: Every referenced interface type is present
TestSpecs.Given: a component whose requirements reference interface names that exist in the model
TestSpecs.Expect: every referenced interface name in the context's requirements resolves to a full InterfaceDefinition in the same context

TestSpecs.Title: Parent system requirements accompany derived requirements
TestSpecs.Given: REQ-001 is a system-level requirement and REQ-001-A derives from it with allocated_to COMP-001
TestSpecs.Expect: AgentContext for COMP-001 contains both REQ-001-A and REQ-001

TestSpecs.Title: System requirements listed in satisfies are included
TestSpecs.Given: COMP-001 has satisfies: REQ-001 and REQ-001 has no derived requirements yet
TestSpecs.Expect: AgentContext for COMP-001 contains REQ-001

TestSpecs.Title: Parent requirement included only once when multiple derived reqs share it
TestSpecs.Given: REQ-001 has two derived requirements both allocated to COMP-001
TestSpecs.Expect: AgentContext for COMP-001 contains REQ-001 exactly once in its requirements list

TestSpecs.Title: AssembleOnly returns only the listed components
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel with three components A, B, C and the affected list is ["A", "C"]
TestSpecs.Expect: the returned list contains AgentContexts for A and C only, with B omitted

TestSpecs.Title: AssembleOnly with empty list returns empty
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel with three components and an empty affected list
TestSpecs.Expect: the returned list is empty

TestSpecs.Title: AssembleOnly ignores unknown component ids
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel with components A and B, and the affected list is ["A", "UNKNOWN"]
TestSpecs.Expect: the returned list contains only the AgentContext for A, and no error occurs

TestSpecs.Title: Added requirement appears in changes
TestSpecs.Given: a baseline with no requirements and a current model containing REQ-001
TestSpecs.Expect: the ChangeSet contains a Change with element_id "REQ-001" and change_type "added"

TestSpecs.Title: Added component appears in changes
TestSpecs.Given: a baseline with no components and a current model containing COMP-001
TestSpecs.Expect: a Change with element_id "COMP-001" and change_type "added"

TestSpecs.Title: Added interface and test spec appear in changes
TestSpecs.Given: a current model adding interface Todo and test TC-001 that are absent from the baseline
TestSpecs.Expect: the ChangeSet contains Change entries for both "Todo" and "TC-001" with change_type "added"

TestSpecs.Title: Changed requirement statement is modified
TestSpecs.Given: a baseline where REQ-001 has statement "X" and a current model where REQ-001 has statement "Y"
TestSpecs.Expect: a Change with element_id "REQ-001" and change_type "modified"

TestSpecs.Title: Source file or line number change alone is not modified
TestSpecs.Given: a baseline and current model that are identical except for source_file and line_number fields
TestSpecs.Expect: the ChangeSet has no modified changes for those elements

TestSpecs.Title: Identical models produce an empty change set
TestSpecs.Given: a baseline and current model that are semantically identical
TestSpecs.Expect: the ChangeSet has no changes and affected_components is empty

TestSpecs.Title: Removed requirement appears in changes
TestSpecs.Given: a baseline containing REQ-001 and a current model that does not contain it
TestSpecs.Expect: a Change with element_id "REQ-001" and change_type "removed"

TestSpecs.Title: Removed component appears in changes
TestSpecs.Given: a baseline with COMP-001 and a current model that does not contain it
TestSpecs.Expect: a Change with element_id "COMP-001" and change_type "removed"

TestSpecs.Title: Directly changed component is affected
TestSpecs.Given: COMP-001 exists in both baseline and current but its responsibility text differs
TestSpecs.Expect: affected_components includes "COMP-001"

TestSpecs.Title: Component allocated to a changed requirement is affected
TestSpecs.Given: REQ-001-A is allocated_to COMP-001 and its statement changed between baseline and current
TestSpecs.Expect: affected_components includes "COMP-001"

TestSpecs.Title: Component satisfying a changed system requirement is affected
TestSpecs.Given: COMP-001 has satisfies: REQ-001 and REQ-001 changed between baseline and current
TestSpecs.Expect: affected_components includes "COMP-001"

TestSpecs.Title: Component that depends_on an affected component is affected
TestSpecs.Given: COMP-002 depends_on COMP-001, and COMP-001 is affected
TestSpecs.Expect: affected_components includes both "COMP-001" and "COMP-002"

TestSpecs.Title: Component verifying a changed test spec is affected
TestSpecs.Given: TC-001 verifies REQ-001-A (allocated_to COMP-001) and the test spec's expect text changed
TestSpecs.Expect: affected_components includes "COMP-001"

TestSpecs.Title: Interface change marks all components as affected
TestSpecs.Given: an interface changed between baseline and current
TestSpecs.Expect: affected_components includes every component in the current model

TestSpecs.Title: SaveBaseline writes the current model to disk
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel and a target baseline path
TestSpecs.Expect: the file exists at the given path and LoadBaseline returns a SystemModel equivalent to the input

TestSpecs.Title: Missing baseline is treated as an empty baseline
TestSpecs.Given: a baseline path that does not exist on disk
TestSpecs.When: Detect is called with that path and a non-empty current model
TestSpecs.Expect: every element of the current model appears in the change set as "added", with no error

TestSpecs.Title: Validate a clean model prints valid and exits zero
TestSpecs.Given: a model directory whose parsed SystemModel has no validation errors
TestSpecs.Expect: stdout includes the word "valid", exit code is 0

TestSpecs.Title: Validate a broken model prints errors and exits non-zero
TestSpecs.Given: a model directory whose parsed SystemModel produces at least one validation error
TestSpecs.Expect: stdout or stderr includes the error message with file path and line number, exit code is non-zero

TestSpecs.Title: Parse error prints file path and exits non-zero
TestSpecs.Given: a model directory where one .req file has a syntax error
TestSpecs.Expect: output contains the offending file name and exit code is non-zero

TestSpecs.Title: Assemble writes one JSON file per component
TestSpecs.Given: a valid model directory with three components and an empty output directory
TestSpecs.Expect: three <component-id>.json files exist under the output directory, exit code is 0

TestSpecs.Title: Assemble refuses when model is invalid
TestSpecs.Given: a model directory whose parsed SystemModel has a validation error
TestSpecs.Expect: no files are written, exit code is non-zero, stderr mentions the validation error

TestSpecs.Title: Serve validates port argument
TestSpecs.Given: an invocation "blueprint serve --port not-a-number"
TestSpecs.Expect: exit code is non-zero and stderr mentions "port"

TestSpecs.Title: Serve rejects missing port value
TestSpecs.Given: an invocation "blueprint serve --port" with no value
TestSpecs.Expect: exit code is non-zero

TestSpecs.Title: Sync with no baseline writes everything and saves baseline
TestSpecs.Given: a valid model directory and no existing baseline file
TestSpecs.When: the user runs "blueprint sync <sys-dir> <out-dir>" from a working directory with no .blueprint/baseline.json
TestSpecs.Expect: AgentContext files are written for every component, a .blueprint/baseline.json is created, and exit code is 0

TestSpecs.Title: Sync with unchanged model writes nothing new
TestSpecs.Given: a baseline that matches the current model
TestSpecs.Expect: stdout reports no changes, no files are regenerated, exit code is 0

TestSpecs.Title: Sync regenerates only affected components
TestSpecs.Given: a baseline and a current model where only one component has changed
TestSpecs.Expect: only the affected component's AgentContext is written and the baseline is updated, exit code is 0

TestSpecs.Title: Forward trace prints satisfied_by and verified_by
TestSpecs.Given: a model directory where REQ-001 is satisfied by COMP-A and verified by TC-001
TestSpecs.Expect: stdout contains "COMP-A" and "TC-001", exit code is 0

TestSpecs.Title: Backward trace from test spec lists requirements
TestSpecs.Given: a model directory where TC-001 verifies REQ-001
TestSpecs.When: the user runs "blueprint trace TC-001 backward <dir>"
TestSpecs.Expect: stdout contains "REQ-001", exit code is 0

TestSpecs.Title: Trace of unknown id exits non-zero
TestSpecs.Given: a model directory with no element id "REQ-NONE"
TestSpecs.Expect: exit code is non-zero

TestSpecs.Title: Verify prints coverage percentage
TestSpecs.Given: a model directory with at least one verified requirement
TestSpecs.Expect: stdout contains a percentage and the coverage summary line

TestSpecs.Title: Verify lists uncovered requirements
TestSpecs.Given: a model directory with one requirement and no test specs
TestSpecs.Expect: stdout lists the uncovered requirement id, exit code is non-zero

TestSpecs.Title: Verify returns zero when fully covered and allocated
TestSpecs.Given: a model where every requirement is verified and every system requirement is satisfied by a component
TestSpecs.Expect: exit code is 0

TestSpecs.Title: Positional directory argument is used
TestSpecs.Given: a valid model directory at path "./custom/sys"
TestSpecs.Expect: the CLI parses that directory (not the default sys/) and exits 0

TestSpecs.Title: Default directory is sys when no argument given
TestSpecs.Given: a working directory containing a valid sys/ folder
TestSpecs.When: the user runs "blueprint validate" with no path
TestSpecs.Expect: the CLI reads from "sys" relative to the working directory and exits 0

TestSpecs.Title: Unknown subcommand prints usage and exits non-zero
TestSpecs.Given: an invocation "blueprint bogus"
TestSpecs.Expect: stderr contains "usage" or "unknown command", exit code is non-zero

TestSpecs.Title: Requirement verified by a test spec is covered
TestSpecs.Given: REQ-001 exists and TC-001 verifies REQ-001
TestSpecs.Expect: the Report.uncovered_requirements list does not include "REQ-001"

TestSpecs.Title: Requirement with no verifying test spec is uncovered
TestSpecs.Given: REQ-002 exists and no test spec verifies it
TestSpecs.Expect: Report.uncovered_requirements includes "REQ-002"

TestSpecs.Title: Applies to derived requirements as well
TestSpecs.Given: a derived requirement REQ-001-A exists and has no verifying test spec
TestSpecs.Expect: Report.uncovered_requirements includes "REQ-001-A"

TestSpecs.Title: System requirement satisfied by a component is allocated
TestSpecs.Given: REQ-001 is a system-level requirement and COMP-A has satisfies: REQ-001
TestSpecs.Expect: Report.unallocated_requirements does not include "REQ-001"

TestSpecs.Title: System requirement with no satisfying component is unallocated
TestSpecs.Given: REQ-002 is a system-level requirement and no component lists it in satisfies
TestSpecs.Expect: Report.unallocated_requirements includes "REQ-002"

TestSpecs.Title: Derived requirements are not checked for allocation
TestSpecs.Given: REQ-001-A is a derived requirement (has derived_from) and no component lists it in satisfies
TestSpecs.Expect: Report.unallocated_requirements does not include "REQ-001-A"

TestSpecs.Title: Total counts every requirement
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel with four requirements (two system-level, two derived)
TestSpecs.Expect: Report.total_requirements: 4

TestSpecs.Title: Covered count reflects verified requirements
TestSpecs.Given: four requirements of which three are verified by at least one test spec
TestSpecs.Expect: Report.covered_requirements: 3

TestSpecs.Title: Coverage percentage is the covered fraction
TestSpecs.Given: four requirements and three covered
TestSpecs.Expect: Report.coverage_percentage is 75.0

TestSpecs.Title: Empty model reports 0 total and 0 percent
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel with no requirements
TestSpecs.Expect: Report.total_requirements: 0, covered_requirements: 0, and coverage_percentage: 0.0

TestSpecs.Title: Parse ubiquitous requirement
TestSpecs.Given: a .req file containing req REQ-001 "Do the thing" priority: must-have The system shall do the thing.
TestSpecs.Expect: one Requirement with id: "REQ-001", title: "Do the thing", priority: "must-have", pattern: "ubiquitous", statement: "The system shall do the thing."

TestSpecs.Title: Parse event-driven requirement
TestSpecs.Given: a .req file containing req REQ-002 "On click" priority: must-have WHEN the user clicks the button, the system shall record the click.
TestSpecs.Expect: Requirement with pattern: "event-driven", statement: "WHEN the user clicks the button, the system shall record the click."

TestSpecs.Title: Parse unwanted-behaviour requirement
TestSpecs.Given: a .req file containing req REQ-003 "Reject empty" priority: must-have IF the input is empty, THEN the system shall reject the request.
TestSpecs.Expect: Requirement with pattern: "unwanted"

TestSpecs.Title: Parse state-driven requirement
TestSpecs.Given: a .req file containing req REQ-004 "While safe" priority: must-have WHILE operating in safe mode, the system shall log all state transitions.
TestSpecs.Expect: Requirement with pattern: "state-driven"

TestSpecs.Title: Parse optional-feature requirement
TestSpecs.Given: a .req file containing req REQ-005 "Admin feature" priority: should-have WHERE admin mode is enabled, the system shall expose the debug panel.
TestSpecs.Expect: Requirement with pattern: "optional"

TestSpecs.Title: Multiple requirements in one file
TestSpecs.Given: a .req file containing three req blocks separated by blank lines
TestSpecs.Expect: three Requirements parsed in file order, each with its own id, title, priority, pattern, and statement

TestSpecs.Title: Multi-line statement collapsed to single string
TestSpecs.Given: a req block whose EARS statement spans three indented lines
TestSpecs.Expect: Requirement.statement equal to the joined text with single spaces between lines and no trailing whitespace

TestSpecs.Title: Priority nice-to-have accepted
TestSpecs.Given: a req block with priority: nice-to-have
TestSpecs.Expect: Requirement.priority: "nice-to-have"

TestSpecs.Title: Acceptance list empty when none given
TestSpecs.Given: a req block with only header, priority, and statement
TestSpecs.Expect: Requirement.accept is an empty list

TestSpecs.Title: Parse interface with primitive fields
TestSpecs.Given: a .iface file containing interface Todo { id: String count: Integer done: Boolean }
TestSpecs.Expect: one InterfaceDefinition named "Todo" with three Fields whose types are "String", "Integer", "Boolean" and whose optional flags are all false

TestSpecs.Title: Parse optional field
TestSpecs.Given: an interface containing a field "note: String?"
TestSpecs.Expect: the Field has type "String" and optional true

TestSpecs.Title: Parse array field
TestSpecs.Given: an interface containing a field "tags: String[]"
TestSpecs.Expect: the Field has type "String[]" and optional false

TestSpecs.Title: Parse union type
TestSpecs.Given: an interface containing a field "status: pending | active | done"
TestSpecs.Expect: the Field has type "pending | active | done"

TestSpecs.Title: Parse reference to another interface type
TestSpecs.Given: an interface containing a field "owner: Component"
TestSpecs.Expect: the Field has type "Component"

TestSpecs.Title: Multiple interfaces in one file
TestSpecs.Given: a .iface file with two interface blocks separated by a blank line
TestSpecs.Expect: two InterfaceDefinitions parsed in file order

TestSpecs.Title: Whitespace alignment in fields accepted
TestSpecs.Given: an interface whose field names are aligned with extra spaces before the colon
TestSpecs.Expect: the Field.name is the identifier only, with no surrounding whitespace

TestSpecs.Title: Parse component with single-line responsibility
TestSpecs.Given: a .component file containing component COMP-001 "Parser" responsibility: Read model files. satisfies: BP-001
TestSpecs.Expect: one Component with id "COMP-001", name "Parser", responsibility "Read model files.", satisfies ["BP-001"], depends_on is an empty list

TestSpecs.Title: Responsibility wraps across indented lines
TestSpecs.Given: a component block whose responsibility spans three indented continuation lines
TestSpecs.Expect: Component.responsibility is the joined text with single spaces between lines

TestSpecs.Title: Parse depends_on list
TestSpecs.Given: a component block with depends_on: COMP-001, COMP-002, COMP-003
TestSpecs.Expect: Component.depends_on: ["COMP-001", "COMP-002", "COMP-003"]

TestSpecs.Title: Parse satisfies list
TestSpecs.Given: a component block with satisfies: BP-002, BP-003
TestSpecs.Expect: Component.satisfies: ["BP-002", "BP-003"]

TestSpecs.Title: Component with no satisfies is accepted
TestSpecs.Given: a component block with responsibility and depends_on but no satisfies line
TestSpecs.Expect: Component.satisfies is an empty list

TestSpecs.Title: Multiple components back-to-back
TestSpecs.Given: a .component file with two component blocks, no blank line between them
TestSpecs.Expect: two Components parsed in file order

TestSpecs.Title: Parse basic test spec
TestSpecs.Given: a .testspec file containing test TC-001 "Do the thing" verifies: REQ-001 given: a condition expect: a result
TestSpecs.Expect: one TestSpec with id "TC-001", title "Do the thing", verifies ["REQ-001"], given "a condition", expect "a result", and when is empty

TestSpecs.Title: Parse test spec with when clause
TestSpecs.Given: a test block that has given, when, and expect clauses
TestSpecs.Expect: TestSpec.when is the when text joined to one line

TestSpecs.Title: Multiple verifies references
TestSpecs.Given: a test block with "verifies: BP-002, BP-003"
TestSpecs.Expect: TestSpec.verifies: ["BP-002", "BP-003"]

TestSpecs.Title: Multi-line given clause
TestSpecs.Given: a test block whose given clause spans two indented continuation lines
TestSpecs.Expect: TestSpec.given is the joined text with single spaces between lines

TestSpecs.Title: Multi-line expect clause
TestSpecs.Given: a test block whose expect clause spans two indented continuation lines
TestSpecs.Expect: TestSpec.expect is the joined text

TestSpecs.Title: Section headers starting with ## are skipped
TestSpecs.Given: a .testspec file with "## BP-001: Some section" lines between test blocks
TestSpecs.Expect: the section lines are ignored and the tests parse successfully

TestSpecs.Title: Multiple tests in one file
TestSpecs.Given: a .testspec file containing three test blocks
TestSpecs.Expect: three TestSpecs parsed in file order

TestSpecs.Title: Parse derived_from and allocated_to
TestSpecs.Given: a .req file containing req BP-001-P01 "Parse reqs" derived_from: BP-001 allocated_to: COMP-001 priority: must-have The parser shall extract requirements.
TestSpecs.Expect: Requirement with derived_from "BP-001", allocated_to "COMP-001", and the expected id, priority, and statement

TestSpecs.Title: Derived fields empty on system-level requirement
TestSpecs.Given: a .req file with no derived_from or allocated_to lines
TestSpecs.Expect: Requirement.derived_from: "" and Requirement.allocated_to: ""

TestSpecs.Title: Derived fields accept ordering either before or after priority
TestSpecs.Given: a req block where allocated_to appears before derived_from and both before priority
TestSpecs.Expect: the parser extracts both fields correctly regardless of order

TestSpecs.Title: Parse full sys directory
TestSpecs.Given: a sys/ directory containing requirements/, interfaces/, components/<name>/, and tests/ subdirectories with valid model files
TestSpecs.Expect: a SystemModel where requirements, interfaces, components, and test_specs lists are all populated from the corresponding files

TestSpecs.Title: Include derived requirements from component folders
TestSpecs.Given: a sys/ directory where components/parser/parser.req contains derived requirements
TestSpecs.Expect: SystemModel.requirements contains both the system-level requirements and the derived requirements

TestSpecs.Title: Missing subdirectory is tolerated
TestSpecs.Given: a sys/ directory that has no tests/ subdirectory
TestSpecs.Expect: ParseSystemModel returns a SystemModel whose test_specs list is empty, with no error

TestSpecs.Title: Empty sys directory returns empty model
TestSpecs.Given: a sys/ directory containing no model files
TestSpecs.Expect: SystemModel with all four lists empty, no error

TestSpecs.Title: Nonexistent path returns error
TestSpecs.Given: a path that does not exist on disk
TestSpecs.Expect: a non-nil error from ParseSystemModel

TestSpecs.Title: Parse error in one file stops traversal and is reported
TestSpecs.Given: a sys/ directory where one .req file has a syntax error
TestSpecs.Expect: a ParseError whose file path points at the offending file

TestSpecs.Title: Source file path recorded
TestSpecs.Given: a .req file at path "sys/requirements/foo.req" containing one req
TestSpecs.Expect: Requirement.source_file ends with "sys/requirements/foo.req"

TestSpecs.Title: Line number points at req header
TestSpecs.Given: a .req file where the req block header appears on line 5
TestSpecs.Expect: Requirement.line_number: 5

TestSpecs.Title: Each requirement keeps its own line number
TestSpecs.Given: a .req file with req headers on lines 1, 7, and 15
TestSpecs.Expect: three Requirements with line_number 1, 7, and 15 respectively

TestSpecs.Title: Interface source location recorded
TestSpecs.Given: a .iface file where an interface header appears on line 3
TestSpecs.Expect: InterfaceDefinition.source_file ends with the file path and InterfaceDefinition.line_number: 3

TestSpecs.Title: Component source location recorded
TestSpecs.Given: a .component file where the component header is on line 2
TestSpecs.Expect: Component.line_number: 2 and source_file ends with the file path

TestSpecs.Title: Test spec source location recorded
TestSpecs.Given: a .testspec file where a test header appears on line 4
TestSpecs.Expect: TestSpec.line_number: 4 and source_file ends with the file path

TestSpecs.Title: Missing req id
TestSpecs.Given: a .req file containing req "No id here" priority: must-have The system shall do something.
TestSpecs.Expect: ParseError with file path, line number, and message indicating an id was expected after "req"

TestSpecs.Title: Missing title
TestSpecs.Given: a req header with an id but no quoted title
TestSpecs.Expect: ParseError whose message mentions "title"

TestSpecs.Title: Unknown priority value
TestSpecs.Given: a req block with priority: urgent
TestSpecs.Expect: ParseError whose message lists the allowed values (must-have, should-have, nice-to-have)

TestSpecs.Title: Missing priority line
TestSpecs.Given: a req block with no priority line before the statement
TestSpecs.Expect: ParseError whose message mentions "priority"

TestSpecs.Title: Empty statement
TestSpecs.Given: a req block with a header and priority but no statement lines
TestSpecs.Expect: ParseError whose message mentions "statement"

TestSpecs.Title: Unterminated title quote
TestSpecs.Given: a req header where the title opens with " but has no closing "
TestSpecs.Expect: ParseError pointing at the header line

TestSpecs.Title: Missing opening brace on interface
TestSpecs.Given: a .iface file with "interface Foo" and no opening brace
TestSpecs.Expect: ParseError pointing at the header line, message mentions "{"

TestSpecs.Title: Missing closing brace on interface
TestSpecs.Given: a .iface file where an interface block opens with { but the file ends before any matching }
TestSpecs.Expect: ParseError whose message mentions "}"

TestSpecs.Title: Malformed field line
TestSpecs.Given: a field line inside an interface with no colon separator
TestSpecs.Expect: ParseError pointing at the offending line, message mentions "field"

TestSpecs.Title: Component missing responsibility
TestSpecs.Given: a component block with a header and satisfies list but no responsibility line
TestSpecs.Expect: ParseError whose message mentions "responsibility"

TestSpecs.Title: Component unknown body key
TestSpecs.Given: a component block containing a line "foo: bar"
TestSpecs.Expect: ParseError pointing at the offending line, message mentions the unknown key

TestSpecs.Title: Test spec missing given
TestSpecs.Given: a test block with verifies and expect but no given clause
TestSpecs.Expect: ParseError whose message mentions "given"

TestSpecs.Title: Test spec missing expect
TestSpecs.Given: a test block with verifies and given but no expect clause
TestSpecs.Expect: ParseError whose message mentions "expect"

TestSpecs.Title: Forward trace returns satisfying components
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel where COMP-A satisfies REQ-001 and COMP-B does not
TestSpecs.When: the Tracer is queried forward with from_id REQ-001
TestSpecs.Expect: the returned chain for REQ-001 has satisfied_by including "COMP-A" and not "COMP-B"

TestSpecs.Title: Forward trace returns verifying test specs
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel where TC-001 verifies REQ-001
TestSpecs.When: the Tracer is queried forward with from_id REQ-001
TestSpecs.Expect: the returned chain for REQ-001 has verified_by including "TC-001"

TestSpecs.Title: Allocated derived requirements count toward satisfying
TestSpecs.Given: a derived requirement REQ-001-A with allocated_to COMP-X, and COMP-X does not list REQ-001-A in satisfies
TestSpecs.When: the Tracer is queried forward with from_id REQ-001-A
TestSpecs.Expect: satisfied_by includes "COMP-X"

TestSpecs.Title: Backward from test spec returns verified requirements
TestSpecs.Given: TC-001 verifies REQ-001 and REQ-002
TestSpecs.When: the Tracer is queried backward with from_id TC-001
TestSpecs.Expect: the TraceResult contains chains for REQ-001 and REQ-002

TestSpecs.Title: Backward from component returns allocated requirements
TestSpecs.Given: COMP-X satisfies REQ-001 and REQ-002-A is allocated_to COMP-X
TestSpecs.When: the Tracer is queried backward with from_id COMP-X
TestSpecs.Expect: the TraceResult contains chains for REQ-001 and REQ-002-A

TestSpecs.Title: Ancestor chain walks derived_from links
TestSpecs.Given: REQ-001 is system-level and REQ-001-A derives from REQ-001
TestSpecs.Expect: the returned chain is ["REQ-001-A", "REQ-001"]

TestSpecs.Title: System-level requirement has a chain of length one
TestSpecs.Given: REQ-001 is a system-level requirement with no derived_from
TestSpecs.Expect: the returned chain is ["REQ-001"]

TestSpecs.Title: Broken derived_from stops the chain
TestSpecs.Given: REQ-X has derived_from REQ-MISSING which does not exist
TestSpecs.Expect: the returned chain stops at REQ-X and does not attempt to follow a missing link

TestSpecs.Title: Matrix contains a chain for every requirement
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel with three requirements
TestSpecs.Expect: the TraceResult contains three chains, one per requirement, in requirements-list order

TestSpecs.Title: Matrix chains include satisfied_by and verified_by
TestSpecs.Given: REQ-001 is satisfied by COMP-A and verified by TC-001
TestSpecs.Expect: the chain for REQ-001 has satisfied_by ["COMP-A"] and verified_by ["TC-001"]

TestSpecs.Title: Valid satisfies resolves
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel where component COMP-001 has satisfies: REQ-001 and REQ-001 exists in the requirements list
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: true

TestSpecs.Title: Broken satisfies reported
TestSpecs.Given: a component with satisfies: REQ-NONEXISTENT
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: false with one error whose message references "satisfies" and "REQ-NONEXISTENT"

TestSpecs.Title: Valid verifies resolves
TestSpecs.Given: a test spec with verifies: REQ-001 and REQ-001 exists
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: true

TestSpecs.Title: Broken verifies reported
TestSpecs.Given: a test spec with verifies: REQ-NONEXISTENT
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: false with an error message referencing "verifies" and "REQ-NONEXISTENT"

TestSpecs.Title: derived_from resolves to system-level requirement
TestSpecs.Given: a derived requirement with derived_from: REQ-001 where REQ-001 is itself a system-level requirement
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: true

TestSpecs.Title: derived_from pointing to another derived req is an error
TestSpecs.Given: a derived requirement REQ-A-01 whose derived_from points at another derived requirement REQ-A (which itself has a derived_from)
TestSpecs.Expect: an error message referencing "derived_from" and noting it must point at a system-level requirement

TestSpecs.Title: derived_from pointing at nothing is an error
TestSpecs.Given: a derived requirement with derived_from: REQ-MISSING
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: false

TestSpecs.Title: allocated_to resolves
TestSpecs.Given: a derived requirement with allocated_to: COMP-001 where COMP-001 exists
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: true

TestSpecs.Title: Broken allocated_to reported
TestSpecs.Given: a derived requirement with allocated_to: COMP-MISSING
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: false with a message referencing "allocated_to" and "COMP-MISSING"

TestSpecs.Title: depends_on resolves
TestSpecs.Given: a component with depends_on: COMP-001 where COMP-001 exists
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: true

TestSpecs.Title: Broken depends_on reported
TestSpecs.Given: a component with depends_on: COMP-MISSING
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: false with a message referencing "depends_on" and "COMP-MISSING"

TestSpecs.Title: Primitive types accepted
TestSpecs.Given: an interface whose fields use String, Integer, Boolean, Float, and Timestamp
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: true

TestSpecs.Title: Reference to another interface accepted
TestSpecs.Given: interface A with a field typed as another interface B, where B is defined in the same model
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: true

TestSpecs.Title: Array base type is checked
TestSpecs.Given: an interface with a field typed as "Unknown[]" where Unknown is neither a primitive nor a defined interface
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: false referencing "Unknown"

TestSpecs.Title: Union types are not resolved against interfaces
TestSpecs.Given: an interface with a field typed as "red | green | blue"
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: true

TestSpecs.Title: Unknown scalar type is reported
TestSpecs.Given: an interface with a field typed as "Widget" where Widget is neither a primitive nor a defined interface
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: false referencing "Widget"

TestSpecs.Title: Duplicate requirement ids reported
TestSpecs.Given: two requirements sharing id "REQ-001"
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: false with an error per occurrence referencing "REQ-001" and "duplicate"

TestSpecs.Title: Duplicate component ids reported
TestSpecs.Given: two components sharing id "COMP-001"
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: false

TestSpecs.Title: Duplicate interface names reported
TestSpecs.Given: two interfaces sharing name "Todo"
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: false

TestSpecs.Title: Duplicate test spec ids reported
TestSpecs.Given: two test specs sharing id "TC-001"
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: false

TestSpecs.Title: Error carries offending element's source location
TestSpecs.Given: a component with a broken satisfies reference located at components/foo/foo.component line 3
TestSpecs.Expect: the ValidationError has source_file ending with "foo.component" and line_number: 3

TestSpecs.Title: Any error marks result invalid
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel producing at least one validation error
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: false

TestSpecs.Title: No errors marks result valid
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel where every cross-reference resolves
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult.valid: true and errors list is empty

TestSpecs.Title: GET /api/model returns the parsed model
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel with components, requirements, interfaces, and test specs
TestSpecs.When: GET /api/model is requested from the running server
TestSpecs.Expect: status 200 and a JSON body whose top-level keys include "components", "requirements", "interfaces", and "test_specs", each a list populated from the model

TestSpecs.Title: Counts match the parsed model
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel with 2 components and 3 requirements
TestSpecs.Expect: the decoded JSON has 2 entries in "components" and 3 entries in "requirements"

TestSpecs.Title: Root path serves the canvas page
TestSpecs.Given: the server is running
TestSpecs.Expect: status 200 and the response body is an HTML document that loads the canvas application

TestSpecs.Title: Clicking a component opens the detail pane
TestSpecs.Given: the top-level canvas is displayed with at least one component node
TestSpecs.When: the user clicks that component node
TestSpecs.Expect: a detail pane is visible alongside the canvas and its header shows the clicked component's id and name

TestSpecs.Title: Clicking the canvas background closes the pane
TestSpecs.Given: the detail pane is open for some component
TestSpecs.When: the user clicks an empty area of the top canvas
TestSpecs.Expect: the detail pane is closed

TestSpecs.Title: List view is the default representation
TestSpecs.Given: the user has just opened the detail pane for a component
TestSpecs.Expect: the pane initially displays the list view

TestSpecs.Title: Toggle switches to canvas view
TestSpecs.Given: the detail pane is showing the list view
TestSpecs.When: the user activates the canvas toggle
TestSpecs.Expect: the pane displays the canvas view for the same component without closing the pane

TestSpecs.Title: Derived reqs render with their testspecs nested
TestSpecs.Given: the detail pane is showing the list view for a component that has derived requirements, each with at least one verifying test spec
TestSpecs.Expect: each derived requirement is rendered with its verifying test specs visually grouped below it

TestSpecs.Title: Component with no derived reqs shows empty state
TestSpecs.Given: a component with no derived requirements
TestSpecs.When: the user opens its detail pane in list view
TestSpecs.Expect: the list view renders an empty-state message instead of an empty section

TestSpecs.Title: Canvas view includes the component and its neighbors
TestSpecs.Given: the detail pane is toggled to canvas view for a component that has derived reqs, satisfied sysreqs, and verifying test specs
TestSpecs.Expect: the sub-canvas renders nodes for the component, its derived requirements, its verifying test specs, and the system requirements it satisfies

TestSpecs.Title: Source path and line appear for selected element
TestSpecs.Given: the detail pane is showing any element (component, requirement, or test spec)
TestSpecs.Expect: the element's source_file path and line_number are visible in the pane

TestSpecs.Title: Server binds and serves on the configured port
TestSpecs.Given: the server is instructed to listen on a free port
TestSpecs.Expect: the server accepts GET /api/model on that port and returns the JSON model

TestSpecs.Title: Default port is 8080
TestSpecs.Given: Serve is invoked with no port override
TestSpecs.Expect: the listen address resolves to :8080

TestSpecs.Title: Parse valid system model
TestSpecs.Given: a sys/ directory containing valid .req, .iface, .component, and .testspec files
TestSpecs.Expect: a structured SystemModel with all elements populated and no errors

TestSpecs.Title: Reject non-text model files
TestSpecs.Given: a sys/ directory containing a binary file with a .req extension
TestSpecs.Expect: a syntax error reported with file path and description

TestSpecs.Title: Handle empty model directory
TestSpecs.Given: a sys/ directory with no model files
TestSpecs.Expect: a valid but empty SystemModel with no errors

TestSpecs.Title: Valid model passes validation
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel where all cross-references resolve
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult{valid: true, errors: []}

TestSpecs.Title: Broken satisfies reference detected
TestSpecs.Given: a component with satisfies: REQ-NONEXISTENT
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult{valid: false} with an error referencing the broken link

TestSpecs.Title: Broken verifies reference detected
TestSpecs.Given: a test spec with verifies: REQ-NONEXISTENT
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult{valid: false} with an error referencing the broken link

TestSpecs.Title: Broken depends_on reference detected
TestSpecs.Given: a component with depends_on: COMP-NONEXISTENT
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationResult{valid: false} with an error referencing the broken link

TestSpecs.Title: Errors include file and line number
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel with a broken reference on line 5 of components/parser/parser.component
TestSpecs.Expect: ValidationError with source_file containing "parser.component" and line_number: 5

TestSpecs.Title: Non-zero exit on validation failure
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel with at least one validation error
TestSpecs.Expect: process exits with non-zero status

TestSpecs.Title: Zero exit on validation success
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel with no validation errors
TestSpecs.Expect: process exits with zero status

TestSpecs.Title: Assemble context for a single component
TestSpecs.Given: a valid SystemModel with one component COMP-001 that satisfies REQ-001, consumes InterfaceA, and is verified by TC-001
TestSpecs.When: context assembly is requested for COMP-001
TestSpecs.Expect: AgentContext containing the component definition, REQ-001, InterfaceA, and TC-001

TestSpecs.Title: Context includes parent system requirements
TestSpecs.Given: a component with derived requirement BP-001-P01 (derived_from: BP-001)
TestSpecs.When: context is assembled for that component
TestSpecs.Expect: AgentContext includes both BP-001-P01 and BP-001

TestSpecs.Title: Context resolves interface references
TestSpecs.Given: a component that consumes InterfaceA, which references InterfaceB in a field type
TestSpecs.When: context is assembled for that component
TestSpecs.Expect: AgentContext includes both InterfaceA and InterfaceB

TestSpecs.Title: Forward trace from requirement
TestSpecs.Given: REQ-001 satisfied by COMP-001, verified by TC-001
TestSpecs.When: forward trace is requested for REQ-001
TestSpecs.Expect: TraceResult showing COMP-001 in satisfied_by and TC-001 in verified_by

TestSpecs.Title: Backward trace from test spec
TestSpecs.Given: TC-001 verifies REQ-001
TestSpecs.When: backward trace is requested for TC-001
TestSpecs.Expect: TraceResult showing REQ-001 as the traced requirement

TestSpecs.Title: Trace follows derived_from chain
TestSpecs.Given: BP-001-P01 derived_from BP-001
TestSpecs.When: backward trace is requested for BP-001-P01
TestSpecs.Expect: TraceResult includes BP-001 as the root system requirement

TestSpecs.Title: Full traceability matrix
TestSpecs.Given: a valid SystemModel with requirements, components, and test specs
TestSpecs.Expect: every requirement appears with its satisfying components and verifying test specs

TestSpecs.Title: Detect uncovered requirement
TestSpecs.Given: REQ-001 has no test spec with verifies: REQ-001
TestSpecs.Expect: CoverageReport with REQ-001 in uncovered_requirements

TestSpecs.Title: Detect unallocated requirement
TestSpecs.Given: REQ-001 has no component with satisfies: REQ-001
TestSpecs.Expect: CoverageReport with REQ-001 in uncovered_requirements

TestSpecs.Title: Full coverage reports 100%
TestSpecs.Given: every requirement is verified by at least one test spec
TestSpecs.Expect: CoverageReport{coverage_percentage: 100.0, uncovered_requirements: []}

TestSpecs.Title: Coverage metrics are accurate
TestSpecs.Given: 4 requirements, 3 covered by test specs, 1 uncovered
TestSpecs.Expect: CoverageReport{total_requirements: 4, covered_requirements: 3, coverage_percentage: 75.0}

TestSpecs.Title: Detect added model element
TestSpecs.Given: a new .req file added since the last sync
TestSpecs.Expect: ChangeSet includes the new requirement as change_type: added

TestSpecs.Title: Detect modified model element
TestSpecs.Given: an existing .component file modified since the last sync
TestSpecs.Expect: ChangeSet includes the component as change_type: modified

TestSpecs.Title: Detect removed model element
TestSpecs.Given: a .req file removed since the last sync
TestSpecs.Expect: ChangeSet includes the requirement as change_type: removed

TestSpecs.Title: Transitive impact detection
TestSpecs.Given: InterfaceA is modified, COMP-001 consumes InterfaceA, COMP-002 depends_on COMP-001
TestSpecs.Expect: ChangeSet affected_components includes both COMP-001 and COMP-002

TestSpecs.Title: Sync regenerates only affected components
TestSpecs.Given: COMP-001 is in the ChangeSet, COMP-003 is not
TestSpecs.Expect: context is assembled for COMP-001 only, COMP-003 is untouched

TestSpecs.Title: Sync with no changes is a no-op
TestSpecs.Given: no model files changed since the last sync
TestSpecs.Expect: ChangeSet{affected_components: [], changes: []} and no context is assembled

TestSpecs.Title: Visual output includes all components
TestSpecs.Given: a SystemModel with 3 components
TestSpecs.Expect: output contains all 3 components with their names

TestSpecs.Title: Visual output shows dependencies
TestSpecs.Given: COMP-002 depends_on COMP-001
TestSpecs.Expect: output shows a dependency link from COMP-002 to COMP-001

TestSpecs.Title: Visual output shows traceability
TestSpecs.Given: COMP-001 satisfies REQ-001, TC-001 verifies REQ-001
TestSpecs.Expect: output shows satisfy and verify links
